architecture.summary: This is a survey paper on watermarking techniques for large language models. It does not propose a single system but reviews and classifies existing watermarking approaches into training-free methods (e.g., post-hoc output manipulation, token constraints) and training-based methods (e.g., embedding watermarks during model training). Information flows from model outputs through watermarking mechanisms that embed verifiable signals for attribution and authentication purposes.

biblio.title: Watermarking for Large Language Models: A Survey

claims.statement: Watermarking techniques have emerged as a promising solution for embedding verifiable signals into model outputs, enabling attribution, authentication, and mitigation of unauthorized usage.
Methods are classified as training-free and training-based approaches with different mechanisms, strengths, and limitations.
Key evaluation criteria for watermarking techniques include robustness, imperceptibility, and payload capacity.
The field lacks a systematic review to consolidate existing research and assess the effectiveness of different techniques.
Key challenges include the absence of a unified taxonomy and limited understanding of trade-offs between capacity, robustness, and imperceptibility in real-world scenarios.
